synopses.01-the-keeper.md:
Aldous, keeper of the light for thirty-one winters, tends the lamp the village thinks him mad to serve.
Tonight, past the shoals, a deliberate dark moves in against the tide — and it is not weather.
synopses.02-the-fog.md:
Fog erases every marker Aldous steers by, the way grief does. He climbs the ninety-two steps unhurried,
certain the light is never lost — only keepers are.
synopses.03-the-light.md:
At the top, lamp against dark, he strikes the match. The beam reaches nineteen miles
and holds the coming thing — pinned, ordinary — in the plain and patient light.
